title: /bin/sh: error while loading shared libraries: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to build a basic container image which is based on python and installs some python packages with pip through podman on Fedora Silverblue:

I've tried the following resolutions:
`podman system reset`
`restorecon -R -F $HOME/.local/share/containers`
`rm -rf $HOME/.local/share/containers`
See issues #10817 and #3234 for the reference to these solutions that I've tried.

`sudo setenforce 0` works but is not desirable for a longterm solution. This basically disables SELinux altogether.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Setup new Silverblue instance, which contains podman by default
2. Try to build a new simple Python container:
My dockerfile
```
FROM python:3.11
WORKDIR /code
COPY ./requirements.txt /code/requirements.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade -r /code/requirements.txt
COPY ./src /src/app
CMD ["uvicorn", "src.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "80", "--reload"]
```

3. Observe above error message instead of the image being built


### Describe the results you received

When building this with the command `podman build -t fastapi-image .`, I notice the following error:
```txt
STEP 1/6: FROM python:3.11
STEP 2/6: WORKDIR /code
--> Using cache a3dd6225ecfee90e444d263e09874abe54f442b0d213de24fb18cb98b55811f8
--> a3dd6225ecfe
STEP 3/6: COPY ./requirements.txt /code/requirements.txt
--> Using cache d8bc34c13415cb4bdd992698846e169d8e2a763404677fae6df1f4a96a39345c
--> d8bc34c13415
STEP 4/6: RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade -r /code/requirements.txt
/bin/sh: error while loading shared libraries: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied
Error: building at STEP "RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade -r /code/requirements.txt": while running runtime: exit status 127
```

### Describe the results you expected

Succesful build of the image without errors or permission issues.

### podman info output


`podman version`:

```yaml
Version:      4.7.0
API Version:  4.7.0
Go Version:   go1.20.8
Built:        Wed Sep 27 20:24:38 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

OS: Fedora Linux 38.20231007.0 (Silverblue) x86_64 
Kernel: 6.5.5-200.fc38.x86_64

Packages:
podman-4.7.0-1.fc38.x86_64

podman-compose-1.0.6-1.fc38.noarch
container-selinux-2.222.0-1.fc38.noarch

### Additional information

Additional commands run:

`sudo ausearch -m avc -ts today`
```txt
time->Tue Oct 10 20:23:08 2023
type=AVC msg=audit(1696962188.806:173): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=6839 comm="sh" path="/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6" dev="dm-0" ino=954459 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c634,c706 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:config_home_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
```

comments.body:
This looks like for some reason container-selinux failed to install properly.

sudo dnf reinstall container-selinux
If this installs properly, then run the restorecon command again and everything should be fine.
It is now working after the command `sudo chcon -Rt container_file_t $HOME/.config/local/share/containers/storage`

> It is now working after the command `sudo chcon -Rt container_file_t $HOME/.config/local/share/containers/storage`

This also worked for me, I got error after changing storage locations in microos, so I ran
sudo chcon -Rt container_file_t /opt/podibag/containerstorage
